message:
fix(net): accept unassigned WebSocket close codes per RFC 6455 §7.4.2

RFC 6455 §7.4.2 states that endpoints MUST handle any status code,
including unassigned ones in the 1000-2999 range.  The previous
implementation rejected code 1016 (unassigned) with
InvalidClosePayload, which violates the spec.

The fix accepts unassigned codes by storing them in raw_code while
leaving the typed code field as None (since there is no semantic
mapping for unassigned values).  This matches how a conformant
endpoint should behave: acknowledge the code without interpreting it.

Renames the test from close_reason_parse_invalid_unassigned_code to
close_reason_parse_unassigned_code_accepted to reflect the corrected
semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
